messages.content: You are a GIS assistant that can help with route optimization, finding nearest amenities, calculate directions from point A to B, shapping polygons of areas as geojsons, respond to geocoding and isochrone question and any other GIS related task. If not specified use PathCheapestArc as a FirstSolutionAlgorithm and GuidedLocalSearch for Metaheuristic with 3 seconds. In route optimization if vehicle type is not specified use Truck as a profile (this is only in route optimization use driving-car or driving-hgv for other tasks). In route optimization if vehicle capacity is not specified use the same value as the load. In route optimization make use of skills property to force a vehicle visiting a certain customer. In route optimization always include id property for all entities, create ids if not provided and make sure they are unique. In route optimization do not specify end location for vehicles unless it is specified by the user. In route optimization make use of end property to make a vehicle drive back to somewhere./nIn route optimization always include all vehicles and customers unless you are asked to exclude some. In route optimization you can change the priority of customers to High, Medium, Low depending on the prompt question even if it is already specified differently, just mention it in the response. Never add comments next to the JSON properties you generate. Do not include properties in the JSON if its value is: null, default, []. Responses should have two separate parts: the DTO and a message where the message is the response to the user.

Can you optimize all current deliveries? Use following data: Current time is 12pm; Current position is -73.64806330848897 45.60304161742206; Vehicle=V1;longitude=-73.5898641;latitude=45.49725132;Load=1000 liters of product 1; Vehicle=V2;longitude=-73.64074913;latitude=45.48627936;Load=1000 liters of product 1 and 1500 of product 2;Capacity=2500 of product 1 and 2500 of product 2; Customer=C1;id=11474;Area=Montreal;Priority=High;longitude=-73.54521924;latitude=45.45419193;Demand=1000 liters of product 1; Customer=C2;id=55474;Area=Longeuil;Priority=Low;longitude=-73.46674187;latitude=45.43982091;Demand=100 liters of product 1 and 1000 liters of product 2; Price of Product 1 = 3$ per liter; Price of Product 2 = 0.5$ per liter.

{"jobs":[{"id":11474,"location":{"longitude":-73.54521924,"latitude":45.45419193},"demands":[{"productId":1,"quantity":1000}],"priority":"High"},{"id":55474,"location":{"longitude":-73.46674187,"latitude":45.43982091},"demands":[{"productId":1,"quantity":100},{"productId":2,"quantity":1000}],"priority":"Medium"}],"vehicles":[{"id":1,"start":{"longitude":-73.5898641,"latitude":45.49725132},"products":[{"id":1,"load":1000,"capacity":2500}]},{"id":2,"start":{"longitude":-73.64074913,"latitude":45.48627936},"products":[{"id":1,"load":1000,"capacity":2500},{"id":2,"load":1500,"capacity":2500}]}],"heuristicParameters":{"firstSolutionAlgorithm":"PathCheapestArc","metaheuristic":"GuidedLocalSearch","timeLimitSeconds":3}}

Can you optimize all high priority deliveries? Use following data: Current time is 7am; Vehicle=V1;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Load=1000 liters of product gas; Vehicle=V2;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Load=7000 liters of product gas and 4400 of product diesel;Capacity=7000 of product gas and 4400 of product diesel; Vehicle=V3;longitude=-73.43386911;latitude=45.36413691;Load=5000 liters of product gas and 4400 of product furnace;Capacity=5000 of product gas and 5000 of product furnace; Vehicle=V4;longitude=-73.58033876;latitude=45.5024504;Load=10000 liters of product gas and 1500 of product furnace and 5000 of product diesel;Capacity=10000 of product diesel and 5000 of product gas and 5000 of furnace; Customer=C1;id=11474;Priority=High;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Demand=5500 liters of product gas; Customer=C2;id=55474;Priority=Low;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Demand=100 liters of product gas and 1000 liters of product furnace.

Can you optimize all deliveries with a demand more than 2000 liters? Use following data: Current time is 8am; Depot=D1;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614; Vehicle=V1;longitude=-73.58033876;latitude=45.5024504;Load=4500 liters of product gas;Capacity=5000 liters of product gas; Vehicle=V2;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Load=3000 liters of product furnace;Capacity=5000 liters of product furnace; Customer=C1;id=11223;Priority=High;longitude=-73.43386911;latitude=45.36413691;Demand=4000 liters of product gas; Customer=C2;id=55678;Priority=Medium;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Demand=1500 liters of product furnace; Price of Product gas = 2.5$ per liter; Price of Product furnace = 3$ per liter.

{"jobs":[{"id":11223,"location":{"longitude":-73.43386911,"latitude":45.36413691},"demands":[{"productId":1,"quantity":4000}],"priority":"High"}],"vehicles":[{"id":"V1","start":{"longitude":-73.58033876,"latitude":45.5024504},"products":[{"id":1,"load":4500,"capacity":5000}]},{"id":"V2","start":{"longitude":-73.44073664,"latitude":45.59886575},"products":[{"id":2,"load":3000,"capacity":5000}]}],"routingOptions":{"profile":"Truck"},"heuristicParameters":{"timeLimitSeconds":3,"firstSolutionAlgorithm":"PathCheapestArc","metaheuristic":"GuidedLocalSearch"}}

Can you dispatch all deliveries where i want vehicle 1 to deliver customer 1 and vehicle 2 to deliver customer 2? Use following data: Current time is 6am; Vehicle=Car-1;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Load=3000 liters of product diesel;Capacity=5000 liters of product diesel; Vehicle=Car-2;longitude=-73.43386911;latitude=45.36413691;Load=4000 liters of product oil;Capacity=8000 liters of product oil; Customer=C1;id=33445;Priority=High;longitude=-73.58033876;latitude=45.5024504;Demand=3500 liters of product diesel; Customer=C2;id=99887;Priority=Low;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Demand=2000 liters of product oil; Customer=C3;id=44567;Priority=Medium;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Demand=1000 liters of product diesel; Price of Product diesel = 2.8$ per liter; Price of Product oil = 1.8$ per liter.

I want to maximize profit, can you dispatch all deliveries by giving priority to higher revenu deliveries? Use following data: Current time is 7am; Depot=D1;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614; Vehicle=V1;longitude=-73.58033876;latitude=45.5024504;Load=500 liters of product oil;Capacity=1000 liters of product oil; Vehicle=V2;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Load=2000 liters of product diesel;Capacity=4000 liters of product diesel; Vehicle=V3;longitude=-73.43386911;latitude=45.36413691;Load=3500 liters of product furnace;Capacity=6000 liters of product furnace; Customer=C1;id=77441;Priority=High;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Demand=3000 liters of product diesel; Customer=C2;id=99867;Priority=Low;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Demand=1500 liters of product furnace; Price of Product oil = 1.5$ per liter; Price of Product diesel = 0.9$ per liter; Price of Product furnace = 3.2$ per liter.

Optimize all current deliveries, note that customer c1 can only receive deliveries start from 2pm to 5pm. Use following data: Current time is 10am; Vehicle=V1;longitude=-73.43386911;latitude=45.36413691;Load=5000 liters of product gas;Capacity=7000 liters of product gas; Vehicle=V2;longitude=-73.58600425999558;latitude=45.498870952285614;Load=2000 liters of product furnace;Capacity=3000 liters of product furnace; Customer=C1;id=44567;Priority=High;longitude=-73.44073664;latitude=45.59886575;Demand=4000 liters of product gas; Customer=C2;id=77889;Priority=Medium;longitude=-73.58033876;latitude=45.5024504;Demand=2000 liters of product furnace; Price of Product gas = 2.4$ per liter; Price of Product furnace = 3.3$ per liter; To calculate the time window we need to know how many seconds left for the customer to receive the delivery. The current time is 10am, so the customer can receive the delivery in 4 hours. 4 hours is equal to 46060 = 14400 seconds from the current time and the limit is 5 pm which is 7 hours from the current time which equals to 76060 = 25200 seconds from the current time.

{"jobs":[{"id":44567,"location":{"longitude":-73.44073664,"latitude":45.59886575},"demands":[{"productId":1,"quantity":4000}],"priority":"High","timeWindows":[{"start":14400,"end":61200}]},{"id":77889,"location":{"longitude":-73.58033876,"latitude":45.5024504},"demands":[{"productId":3,"quantity":2000}],"priority":"Medium"}],"vehicles":[{"id":"V1","start":{"longitude":-73.43386911,"latitude":45.36413691},"products":[{"id":1,"load":5000,"capacity":7000}]},{"id":"V2","start":{"longitude":-73.58600425999558,"latitude":45.498870952285614},"products":[{"id":3,"load":2000,"capacity":3000}]}],"routingOptions":{"profile":"Truck"},"heuristicParameters":{"timeLimitSeconds":3,"firstSolutionAlgorithm":"PathCheapestArc","metaheuristic":"GuidedLocalSearch"}}
